title: Implement azq form build as a thin command-layer writer
goal: Make azq/formam/build.py coordinate parent-goal validation, stub deliverable creation, and canonical Formam writes without turning Formam into a task system.
context: Wave A established canonical Formam storage, normalization, parsing, and write helpers in azq/formam/storage.py.
The implementation plan explicitly says azq form build <goal_id> should generate a stub deliverable and a goal map rather than trying to solve full planning on day one.
The live CLI still has no form command branch, so build.py should remain a small command-layer module that can be wired later.
Formam must define deliverables and boundaries of work, not execution tasks.
requirements: Create azq/formam/build.py.
Add a small command-layer entrypoint such as build_form(goal_id).
Validate the parent goal by exact goal_id through the canonical Finis-backed Formam validation helper.
Allocate the next stable DELIV_### id through storage.py.
Generate one deterministic stub deliverable record from the validated goal data.
Write one canonical deliverable file and one canonical goal map file through storage.py.
Keep the implementation deterministic, inspectable, and free of Agenda-style task generation.
acceptance_criteria: A valid canonical goal can produce one DELIV_###.md file and one GOAL_<goal_id>_MAP.md file.
build.py delegates persistence decisions to azq/formam/storage.py rather than writing files ad hoc.
The initial build behavior remains intentionally minimal and human-readable.
suggested_commit_message: add form build command module for canonical deliverables

title: Implement azq form list as a thin presentation layer
goal: Make azq/formam/deliverables.py provide a read-only listing view over canonical Formam storage.
context: The implementation plan calls for azq form list as one of the first public Formam commands.
Wave B should expose read commands only after the write path exists, so list should sit on top of the canonical storage foundation and the first build path.
The CLI still does not know about form commands, so this task should stay focused on presentation logic rather than command wiring.
requirements: Create azq/formam/deliverables.py.
Add a read-only listing function such as list_deliverables().
Load canonical deliverables through azq/formam/storage.py.
Display at least deliverable_id, goal_id, title, and status.
Keep the output simple and terminal-readable.
Preserve a clear no-deliverables message when canonical storage is empty.
acceptance_criteria: deliverables.py can present canonical deliverables without mutating state.
The listing reads through storage.py rather than duplicating repository logic.
The visible output stays focused on deliverables, not tasks or scheduling.

title: Implement azq form show with exact-id deliverable inspection
goal: Extend azq/formam/deliverables.py so one canonical deliverable can be inspected clearly by exact deliverable_id.
context: The implementation plan calls for azq form show <deliverable_id> as a first-class Formam read command.
Exact-id lookup matters because later Agenda work will depend on stable deliverable identifiers.
This command should inspect one existing deliverable, not broaden into search, filtering, or planning features.
requirements: Add a reader such as show_deliverable(deliverable_id).
Load one deliverable by exact deliverable_id through storage.py.
Print the canonical deliverable fields clearly, including dependencies and artifact_description.
Handle missing deliverable ids with a readable exact-id failure message.
Keep output centered on artifact definition rather than task speculation.
acceptance_criteria: One canonical deliverable can be inspected by exact id.
Missing deliverables fail clearly without fuzzy matching.
deliverables.py remains a presentation module over shared storage helpers.
suggested_commit_message: add exact deliverable inspection for form show

title: Implement azq form map as a thin command-layer map refresher
goal: Make azq/formam/maps.py regenerate a canonical goal map from the current deliverables for one exact goal.
context: The implementation plan calls for azq form map <goal_id> to create or refresh the visible dependency structure for a goal.
Wave A already defined canonical map storage and write helpers, so Wave B should build a thin command-layer refresher on top of that foundation.
Map output should remain human-readable and conservative even when dependency data is sparse.
requirements: Create azq/formam/maps.py.
Add a command-layer entrypoint such as build_goal_map(goal_id) or refresh_goal_map(goal_id).
Validate the parent goal by exact goal_id through the canonical Formam validation helper.
Load deliverables for that exact goal through storage.py.
Write or refresh one canonical GOAL_<goal_id>_MAP.md file through storage.py.
If dependency edges are derived automatically, derive them conservatively from existing deliverable dependency lists rather than inventing planning structure.
Do not create tasks or broaden into Agenda behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A goal with deliverables can produce or refresh one visible canonical map artifact.
Map regeneration depends on canonical deliverables for the exact parent goal.
maps.py remains a thin command-layer module over storage.py.
suggested_commit_message: add form goal map command module

title: Wire Formam commands into the CLI
goal: Expose the first usable Formam loop in azq/cli.py without broadening beyond the Stage 2 command set.
context: The live CLI currently exposes Scintilla and Finis commands only.
The implementation plan defines the first Formam command set as azq form build, azq form list, azq form show, and azq form map.
Canonical goals already depend on ensure_finis_storage_ready(), so Formam command routing should keep goal-backed operations aligned with the Stage 1 migration boundary.
requirements: Add command help lines for azq form build <goal_id>, azq form list, azq form show <deliverable_id>, and azq form map <goal_id>.
Add a form branch in azq/cli.py with clear usage messages for each supported subcommand.
Route build, list, show, and map to the new azq/formam modules.
Run ensure_finis_storage_ready() before Formam commands that depend on canonical goals.
Keep command handling narrow and stylistically consistent with the existing CLI.
Do not introduce Agenda, doctor, or status behavior in this task.
acceptance_criteria: The CLI exposes the first usable Formam command loop.
azq form build, azq form list, azq form show, and azq form map dispatch to dedicated Formam modules.
CLI help and usage text stay aligned with the current Stage 2 scope.
